text.title: Go Goroutines and Worker Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Goroutines and Worker Patterns application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every goroutine MUST have a documented exit (channel close / `ctx.Done` / finite loop).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go memory model + reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Long-running goroutines MUST honour `context.Context` cancellation via `select`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go context docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Each goroutine started by the service MUST be tracked in a `sync.WaitGroup`; main waits before exit.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: stdlib sync docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Shared state MUST be guarded by `sync.Mutex` / `sync.RWMutex` or owned by one goroutine and accessed via channels.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go memory model. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Goroutines MUST recover panics; un-recovered panic kills the process.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: stdlib + Go FAQ. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Goroutine count MUST be bounded by a semaphore or worker pool, never `for { go ... }` unbounded.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Effective Go. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Components spawning background goroutines MUST ship a `goleak.VerifyNone(t)` test.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: goleak docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
